revision.text: '''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への給湯・排水方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させろ過などをし、塩素系の薬剤を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。

'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されてる方式である。 === 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 === *明確な区分は無いものの、浴槽の水を直接排水溝に流してれば掛け流しの可能性がある。 **浴槽の周辺に、お湯を集めて排水していれば、循環風呂の可能性がある。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給してる。
=== 循環風呂の利点 === *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。
=== 循環風呂の欠点 === *レジオネラ菌繁殖を抑える為、塩素系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を濾過運転させなければならない。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、色素等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

{{出典の明記|date=2009年1月}} '''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されてる方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *明確な区分は無いものの、浴槽の水を直接排水溝に流してれば掛け流しの可能性がある。 **浴槽の周辺に、お湯を集めて排水していれば、循環風呂の可能性がある。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給してる。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

revision.comment: /* 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 */
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2009年1月}} '''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されてる方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *明確な区分は無いものの、浴槽の水を直接排水溝に流していれば掛け流しの可能性がある。 **浴槽の周辺に、お湯を集めて排水していれば、循環風呂の可能性がある。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給してる。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。

'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *明確な区分は無いものの、浴槽の水を直接排水溝に流していれば掛け流しの可能性がある。 **浴槽の周辺に、お湯を集めて排水していれば、循環風呂の可能性がある。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。

== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過|濾過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *明確な区分は無いものの、浴槽の水を直接排水溝に流していれば掛け流しの可能性がある。 **浴槽の周辺に、お湯を集めて排水していれば、循環風呂の可能性がある。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。
== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の1つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯を、オーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過|濾過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。 一般的に、[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *施設により形態が異なるため、明確な区分は無いが以下の点で判別できる。 **浴槽の底部にある集水溝から常時集水し、また湯の供給量に対し湯が溢れ出ていない場合は原則として循環風呂である。 **オーバーフローしていても、浴槽全体から無作為にオーバーフローしている場合は原則掛け流しであるが、特定の場所（浴槽の縁の凹部）から溢れ、かつ浴槽の水以外（洗い場の水、雨水、他浴槽の水等）が入り込まない様に集水していれば循環形式の可能性がある。 **露天風呂に雨が降らない様に屋根があるケースは、循環形式の可能性が高い。 **また、塩素系消毒剤が強い場合も循環風呂である。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している場合もある。 **また火山地帯にある一軒宿でも無いにも関わらず、数多くのホテル旅館が林立する温泉街での100名程度入浴できる様な大きい大浴場も、原則として循環である。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。 **ただし源泉掛け流しの場合、雨水扱いとなるため下水道料金は発生しない。 *温泉の枯渇の防止。 *大人数が入る場合などの衛生管理が保持できる点。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 **この濾過をするため、温泉の有効成分も損なわれてしまう。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。 **この塩素系薬剤の匂いで、本来の温泉特有の香りが失われる。
== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の一つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯をオーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過|濾過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。一般的に[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *施設により形態が異なるため、明確な区分は無いが以下の点で判別できる。 **浴槽の底部にある集水溝から常時集水し、また湯の供給量に対し湯が溢れ出ていない場合は原則として循環風呂である。 **オーバーフローしていても、浴槽全体から無作為にオーバーフローしている場合は原則掛け流しであるが、特定の場所（浴槽の縁の凹部）から溢れ、かつ浴槽の水以外（洗い場の水、雨水、他浴槽の水等）が入り込まない様に集水していれば循環形式の可能性がある。 **露天風呂に雨が降らない様に屋根があるケースは、循環形式の可能性が高い。 **また、塩素系消毒剤が強い場合も循環風呂である。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している場合もある。 **また火山地帯にある一軒宿でも無いにも関わらず、数多くのホテル旅館が林立する温泉街での100名程度入浴できる様な大きい大浴場も、原則として循環である。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。 **ただし源泉掛け流しの場合、雨水扱いとなるため下水道料金は発生しない。 *温泉の枯渇の防止。 *大人数が入る場合などの衛生管理が保持できる点。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 **この濾過をするため、温泉の有効成分も損なわれてしまう。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。 **この塩素系薬剤の匂いで、本来の温泉特有の香りが失われる。
== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]

revision.text: #redirect[[:en:recirculating bath]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:循環風呂]]

'''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of {{ill|Hot spring|de|???|ja|温泉|zh|???}} {{ill|Hot water supply facility|de|???|ja|給湯設備|zh|???|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|de|???|ja|排水設備|zh|???|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水}} to the {{ill|Bath|de|???|ja|風呂|zh|???|lt=bathtub|lt_ja=浴槽}}. Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|de|???|ja|源泉|zh|???}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing {{ill|Filtration|de|???|ja|ろ過|zh|???|lt=filtration|lt_ja=濾過}}, and mixed with a {{ill|Chlorine|de|???|ja|塩素|zh|???}}-based {{ill|Disinfectant|de|???|ja|消毒薬|zh|???|lt=chemicals|lt_ja=薬剤}} before returning again to the {{ill|Bath|de|???|ja|風呂|zh|???|lt=bathtub|lt_ja=浴槽}}. It contrasts with {{ill|Kakenagashi (continuous flow)|de|???|ja|掛け流し|zh|???}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|Day-trip bathing facility|de|???|ja|日帰り入浴施設|zh|???}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Kakenagashi == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Kakenagashi; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Kakenagashi (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|de|???|ja|レジオネラ菌|zh|???}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
[[Category:Hot springs]] {{Onsen-stub}} [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:循環風呂]][[de:Zirkulationsbad]]
[[ja:循環風呂]]

This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|Day-trip bathing facility|de|???|ja|日帰り入浴施設|zh|???}}.

== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Kakenagashi (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|de|???|ja|レジオネラ菌|zh|???}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

'''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of {{ill|Hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|12=draft}} {{ill|Hot water supply facility|ja|給湯設備|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯|12=draft}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|ja|排水設備|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|lt=bathtub|lt_ja=浴槽|12=draft}}. Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|12=draft}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing {{ill|Filtration|ja|ろ過|lt=filtration|lt_ja=濾過|12=draft}}, and mixed with a {{ill|Chlorine|ja|塩素|12=draft}}-based {{ill|Disinfectant|ja|消毒薬|lt=chemicals|lt_ja=薬剤|12=draft}} before returning again to the {{ill|Bath|ja|風呂|lt=bathtub|lt_ja=浴槽|12=draft}}. It contrasts with {{ill|Kakenagashi (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|12=draft}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|Day-trip bathing facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=draft}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Kakenagashi == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Kakenagashi; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Kakenagashi (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=draft}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
{{Onsen-stub}} [[category:Completion tier 1]]
{{Onsen}}

[[ja:循環風呂]] [[de:Zirkulationsbad]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:循環風呂 up to rev 95127553
revision.text:
'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の一つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯をオーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過|濾過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。一般的に[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *施設により形態が異なるため、明確な区分は無いが以下の点で判別できる。 **浴槽の底部にある集水溝から常時集水し、また湯の供給量に対し湯が溢れ出ていない場合は原則として循環風呂である。 **オーバーフローしていても、浴槽全体から無作為にオーバーフローしている場合は原則掛け流しであるが、特定の場所（浴槽の縁の凹部）から溢れ、かつ浴槽の水以外（洗い場の水、雨水、他浴槽の水等）が入り込まない様に集水していれば循環形式の可能性がある。 **露天風呂に雨が降らない様に屋根があるケースは、循環形式の可能性が高い。 **また、塩素系消毒剤が強い場合も循環風呂である。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している場合もある。 **また火山地帯にある一軒宿でも無いにもかかわらず、数多くのホテル旅館が林立する温泉街での100名程度入浴できる様な大きい大浴場も、原則として循環である。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。 **ただし源泉掛け流しの場合、雨水扱いとなるため下水道料金は発生しない。 *温泉の枯渇の防止。 *大人数が入る場合などの衛生管理が保持できる点。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 **この濾過をするため、温泉の有効成分も損なわれてしまう。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。 **この塩素系薬剤の匂いで、本来の温泉特有の香りが失われる。
== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[循環風呂]] to [[Recirculating Baths]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''循環風呂'''（じゅんかんぶろ）とは、[[温泉]]の[[風呂|浴槽]]への[[給湯設備|給湯]]・[[排水設備|排水]]方法の一つで、[[源泉]]から自然に湧出または機械的に汲み上げた温泉を浴槽に供給し、浴槽から溢れ出た湯をオーバーフロー槽（ピット）に貯めて循環させ[[ろ過|濾過]]などをし、[[塩素]]系の[[消毒薬|薬剤]]を混ぜて再び[[風呂|浴槽]]に戻すこと。[[掛け流し]]と対をなす言葉である。一般的に[[日帰り入浴施設]]などで採用されている方式である。
== 循環風呂と掛け流しの見分け方 == *施設により形態が異なるため、明確な区分は無いが以下の点で判別できる。 **浴槽の底部にある集水溝から常時集水し、また湯の供給量に対し湯が溢れ出ていない場合は原則として循環風呂である。 **オーバーフローしていても、浴槽全体から無作為にオーバーフローしている場合は原則掛け流しであるが、特定の場所（浴槽の縁の凹部）から溢れ、かつ浴槽の水以外（洗い場の水、雨水、他浴槽の水等）が入り込まない様に集水していれば循環形式の可能性がある。 **露天風呂に雨が降らない様に屋根があるケースは、循環形式の可能性が高い。 **また、塩素系消毒剤が強い場合も循環風呂である。 **地域により若干の違いはあるが、循環風呂の場合は浴槽内部より必ずお湯を供給している場合もある。 **また火山地帯にある一軒宿でも無いにもかかわらず、数多くのホテル旅館が林立する温泉街での100名程度入浴できる様な大きい大浴場も、原則として循環である。
== 循環風呂の利点 == *濾過・循環をする事により、源泉のお湯の節約や下水道料金などの経費節減になる。 **ただし源泉掛け流しの場合、雨水扱いとなるため下水道料金は発生しない。 *温泉の枯渇の防止。 *大人数が入る場合などの衛生管理が保持できる点。
== 循環風呂の欠点 == *[[レジオネラ菌]]繁殖を抑える為、[[塩素]]系の薬剤を投与し残留塩素を一定時間指定値を[[濾過]]運転させなければならない。 **この濾過をするため、温泉の有効成分も損なわれてしまう。 *塩素系薬剤を使用すると、[[色素]]等が無効になるため季節の替わり湯などが行いにくくなる。 **この塩素系薬剤の匂いで、本来の温泉特有の香りが失われる。
== 参考文献 == *温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (光文社新書) *これは、温泉ではない (光文社新書)
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html 横浜市衛生研究所：24時間風呂を使っているのですが...]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆんかんふろ}} [[Category:温泉]] {{Onsen-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:循環風呂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11489431]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] {{ill|Hot water supply facility|ja|給湯設備|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot water supply facility|qq=|1=Hot water supply facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606764}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|ja|排水設備|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drainage facility|qq=|1=drainage equipment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124624624}} to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source spring|qq=|1=Spring Mouth|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564196}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing [[:en:Filtration|filtration]], and mixed with a [[:en:Chlorine|Chlorine]]-based [[:en:Disinfectant|chemicals]] before returning again to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. It contrasts with {{ill|Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{Onsen}}
{{translated page|ja|循環風呂|version=95127553|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source spring|qq=|1=Spring Mouth|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564196}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing [[:en:Filtration|filtration]], and mixed with a [[:en:Chlorine|Chlorine]]-based [[:en:Disinfectant|chemicals]] before returning again to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. It contrasts with {{ill|Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

[[ja:循環風呂]] [[de:Zirkulationsbad]] [[Category:Hot_springs_of_Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11489431}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:循環風呂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11489431]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] {{ill|Hot water supply facility|ja|給湯設備|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot water supply facility|1=Hot water supply facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606764}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|ja|排水設備|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drainage facility|1=drainage equipment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124624624}} to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source spring|1=Spring Mouth|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564196}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing [[:en:Filtration|filtration]], and mixed with a [[:en:Chlorine|Chlorine]]-based [[:en:Disinfectant|chemicals]] before returning again to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. It contrasts with {{ill|Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{Onsen}}

Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source spring|1=Spring Mouth|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564196}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing [[:en:Filtration|filtration]], and mixed with a [[:en:Chlorine|Chlorine]]-based [[:en:Disinfectant|chemicals]] before returning again to the [[:en:Furo|bathtub]]. It contrasts with {{ill|Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}.
== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
{{Onsen-stub}}
{{Onsen}}
{{translated page|ja|循環風呂|version=95127553|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Onsens]] }}

== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:循環風呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:循環風呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:循環風呂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11489431]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} {{ill|Hot water supply facility|ja|給湯設備|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot water supply facility|1=Hot water supply facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606764}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|ja|排水設備|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drainage facility|1=drainage equipment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124624624}} to the {{ill|Furo|en|Furo|WD=Q2637646|lt=bathtub}}. Natural or mechanically pumped {{ill|Source spring|ja|源泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Source spring|1=Spring Mouth|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564196}} water is supplied to the bathtub, and the overflow is collected into an overflow tank (pit). The water is then recirculated, undergoing {{ill|Filtration|en|Filtration|WD=Q244772|lt=filtration}}, and mixed with a {{ill|Chlorine|en|Chlorine|WD=Q688|lt=Chlorine}}-based {{ill|Disinfectant|en|Disinfectant|WD=Q73984|lt=chemicals}} before returning again to the {{ill|Furo|en|Furo|WD=Q2637646|lt=bathtub}}. It contrasts with {{ill|Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|ja|掛け流し|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath (continuous flow)|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}. This recirculation system is commonly used in facilities such as {{ill|1-day onsen facility|ja|日帰り入浴施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}}.

== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

== How to Distinguish Junkanburo from Flowing Bath == * Facility layouts vary, so there is no absolute rule, but the following points can be helpful: ** If water is constantly being drawn from a drain at the bottom of the tub, and if the supply of water is not causing any noticeable overflow, it is likely to be a Junkanburo system. ** Even if there is overflow, if it flows randomly over the entire tub edge, it is generally Flowing Bath; however, if it overflows only at a specific location (a recessed edge of the bathtub), where water from outside the bathtub (washing area water, rainwater, water from other tubs, etc.) does not enter, it may still be a recirculating system. ** In open-air baths with a roof designed to exclude rain, there is a higher likelihood of a Junkanburo system. ** If a strong chlorine smell is present, this also indicates a Junkanburo. ** Although there are regional differences, in many Junkanburo setups, the hot water is always supplied from inside the tub itself. ** In an area not known for abundant volcanic springs—especially a busy hot-spring town with many hotels and ryokan close together—large communal baths accommodating around 100 people are usually Junkanburo.
== Advantages of Junkanburo == * By filtering and recirculating, operators can conserve spring water and reduce costs such as sewage fees. ** Note that in the case of true Flowing Bath (continuous flow) from a natural source, the used water may be treated as rainwater, so sewage fees do not apply. * Helps prevent depletion of the spring source. * Makes it easier to maintain hygiene standards, especially when handling large numbers of bathers.
== Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]

'''Junkanburo''' (循環風呂) is one method of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} {{ill|Hot water supply facility|ja|給湯設備|lt=hot water supply|lt_ja=給湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot water supply facility|1=hot water supply|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11606764}} / {{ill|Drainage facility|ja|排水設備|lt=drainage|lt_ja=排水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drainage facility|1=drainage equipment|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124624624}} to the {{ill|Furo|en|Furo|WD=Q2637646|lt=bathtub|1=Furo}}. The water is then recirculated, undergoing {{ill|Filtration|en|Filtration|WD=Q244772|lt=filtration|1=Filtration}}, and mixed with a {{ill|Chlorine|en|Chlorine|WD=Q688|lt=Chlorine|1=Chlorine}}-based {{ill|Disinfectant|en|Disinfectant|WD=Q73984|lt=chemicals|1=Disinfectant}} before returning again to the {{ill|Furo|en|Furo|WD=Q2637646|lt=bathtub|1=Furo}}. == Disadvantages of Junkanburo == * To prevent the proliferation of {{ill|Legionella|ja|レジオネラ菌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Legionella|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to レジオネラ|1=Legionella|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q158013}}, chlorine-based chemicals must be used, maintaining a prescribed level of residual chlorine during filtration cycles. ** This filtration process can also remove beneficial mineral components inherent in the hot spring. * When chlorine-based agents are used, any added pigments (for seasonal “themed” baths, etc.) become ineffective. ** The distinctive aroma of natural hot spring water is often masked by the smell of chlorine chemicals.
== References == * 温泉教授の温泉ゼミナール (Kobunsha Shinsho) * これは、温泉ではない (Kobunsha Shinsho)
== External Links == * [http://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kenko/eiken/life-inf/mizu/24bath.html Yokohama City Institute of Health: "I’m using a 24-hour bath, but..."]
